skill.name: study-limitations-drafter
skill.description: Generates professionally worded study limitation statements for academic manuscripts, grant proposals, and peer review responses.
skill.summary: Transforms study design flaws into professionally worded limitation statements with balanced tone and mitigation strategies.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 47 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' findings are markdown code blocks documenting legitimate Python script execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are string literal templates in the Python script and SKILL.md, not actual cryptographic code. The 'path traversal' findings are from a security checklist documenting best practices. The Python script (scripts/main.py) is a simple academic writing tool that writes user-specified output files and has no network access, no environment variable access, and no command injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Markdown code formatting incorrectly flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick-wrapped text in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as shell command execution. In all 27 instances, the backticks are standard markdown formatting for code spans and code blocks showing example commands like python scripts/main.py. No dynamic or user-controlled shell execution occurs. The skill does document legitimate Python script invocation, which is expected for a skill with a packaged executable.

low_findings.title: String literals incorrectly flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged string content in scripts/main.py and SKILL.md as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are plain-text limitation statement templates (e.g., 'The retrospective nature of this study introduces potential selection bias') and documentation text. No cryptographic operations, key material, or hashing functions exist anywhere in the codebase.

low_findings.title: Security checklist entries incorrectly flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md lines 117 and 120 as 'Path traversal sequence'. These lines are part of a Security Checklist documenting that file system access should be restricted. The text '(../)' appears in checklist items describing what to prevent, not in actual path construction code.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL incorrectly flagged as network endpoint
low_findings.description: The GitHub source repository URL in SKILL.md line 7 was flagged as a network endpoint. This is a static informational hyperlink to the skill's source repository, not executable network code. The Python script has no network calls, no HTTP imports, and no URL-based operations.

low_findings.title: Audit result JSON content incorrectly flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Text content in study-limitations-drafter_audit_result_v2.json was flagged as system reconnaissance. These are pre-computed audit results containing evaluation text and assertion outcomes. No system enumeration, host discovery, or information gathering behavior exists in any skill file.

content.user_title: Draft Study Limitations for Academic Papers
content.value_statement: Academic writers struggle to articulate study limitations without undermining their research contribution. This skill transforms study design flaws into professionally worded limitation statements with balanced tone and integrated mitigation strategies.
actual_capabilities: Generates professionally worded limitation paragraphs from study design constraints
Supports five common limitation types: sample size, retrospective design, single-center study, short follow-up, and missing data
Produces balanced tone statements that acknowledge limitations without undermining research value
Saves generated text to file for direct use in manuscripts via the --output parameter
Includes mitigation context within each limitation statement to demonstrate methodological rigor
limitations: Limited to five predefined limitation templates; cannot generate custom limitation types without manual template extension in Python code
Does not perform statistical analysis or quantify the actual impact of limitations on study results
Output requires human review and adaptation for field-specific terminology and journal-specific formatting requirements
Templates use general medical research language and may need adjustment for non-medical academic disciplines

use_cases.title: Drafting Manuscript Limitation Sections
use_cases.description: Researchers writing journal articles use this skill to transform study design constraints into professionally worded limitation paragraphs with appropriate academic tone.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researchers preparing manuscripts for peer-reviewed journals

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Risk Assessment
use_cases.description: Grant applicants acknowledge methodological constraints in funding proposals with balanced language that demonstrates awareness without undermining the proposal.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and academics preparing grant applications

use_cases.title: Peer Review Response Preparation
use_cases.description: Authors responding to reviewer comments about study weaknesses generate professionally framed responses that address concerns constructively.
use_cases.target_user: Manuscript authors revising papers after peer review

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Single Limitation Statement
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a limitation statement for a {limitation_type} issue in my study.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have one specific limitation to address, such as sample_size, retrospective, single_center, short_followup, or missing_data

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Complete Limitations Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a limitations section for my study addressing these issues: {list_of_issues}. Format as a professional academic subsection with each limitation in its own paragraph.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a full limitations subsection for a manuscript with multiple constraints to document

prompt_templates.title: Generate Limitations with Mitigation Strategies
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a limitations section addressing {issues}. For each limitation, include specific steps taken to mitigate its impact on study validity.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when peer reviewers have requested detailed mitigation information alongside each acknowledged limitation

prompt_templates.title: Review and Polish Generated Limitations
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the following limitation statements for academic tone, clarity, and balance. Suggest improvements to ensure each limitation is honest but does not undermine the study contribution: {text}
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after generating initial limitation text to refine language and ensure appropriate framing

output_examples.input: Generate a limitation statement for a single-center study design.
output: Findings from this single-center study may not generalize to other populations or healthcare settings. Future multi-center research would help validate these results across diverse clinical environments.

output_examples.input: Draft limitations for small sample size and short follow-up period.
output: The sample size in this study was limited, which may have reduced statistical power to detect smaller effects between groups.
The relatively short follow-up period limits assessment of long-term outcomes and delayed effects. Extended observation periods in future studies would provide more definitive evidence.
Despite these limitations, this study provides valuable preliminary evidence that informs future research directions.

best_practices: Specify all relevant limitation types in a single request to generate a complete limitations section in one pass
Review each generated statement against your actual study design to ensure accurate representation of your specific context
Pair each acknowledged limitation with a concrete mitigation strategy to maintain a balanced and credible scientific tone
anti_patterns: Do not use generated statements verbatim without reviewing for study-specific accuracy and journal formatting requirements
Do not list every possible limitation; focus on the most relevant constraints that could reasonably affect interpretation of results
Do not frame limitations in a way that undermines the core contribution of your work; maintain a constructive and balanced perspective

faq.question: What limitation types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It currently supports five types: small sample size, retrospective design, single-center study, short follow-up period, and missing data.

faq.question: Can I add custom limitation types?
faq.answer: The predefined templates cover common limitation types. To add custom types, you would need to edit the LIMITATION_TEMPLATES dictionary in scripts/main.py.

faq.question: Does this skill perform statistical power analysis?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates text statements only. It does not calculate, validate, or verify any statistical measures.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-medical research?
faq.answer: The templates use general medical research language. You should review and adapt the output for your specific academic field.

faq.question: Can I save the output to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --output parameter when running the script to save the limitations section directly to a file.

faq.question: How should I integrate this into my writing workflow?
faq.answer: Use it after completing your study analysis. List your study limitations, generate the section, then review and customize the output for your specific manuscript and target journal.
